name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-43
title: tdd-step.py main accepts cmd values not in usage list
description: tdd-step.py main treats empty cmd or '-h'/'--help'/'help' specially (lines 378-380), printing usage and returning 0. But the usage() function never lists 'help' as a valid subcommand. Either (a) document 'help' as a real subcommand in usage(), or (b) remove the special-case and let unknown-subcommand handling cover it. Currently behaviour and docs disagree.
